intro: Professionals searching for reporting currency vs consolidation currency are usually separating entity-level presentation from group-level assembly. Reporting currency presents measured results at State 06; consolidation currency assembles multiple entity packages, eliminations, ownership effects, and group reporting at State 07. The distinction matters because a subsidiary reporting pack is not the same as a consolidated currency layer.
doctrine_statement: Reporting currency presents an entity view. Consolidation currency assembles a group view.
primary_section_body: Reporting currency belongs to the entity or reporting package that presents measured results to a defined audience.
Consolidation currency belongs to the group process that combines entities, applies eliminations, handles ownership changes, and produces consolidated statements.
The two states are adjacent, but consolidation adds governance that does not exist inside a single reporting pack.

pillar_sections.title: Side-by-side distinction
paragraphs: Use this section as the quick comparison layer: what each term means, where it sits in the currency-state chain, and which evidence proves it.

steps.title: Reporting currency
steps.body: State 06. The presentation lens for measured results at entity, management, statutory, or reporting-package level.

steps.title: Consolidation currency
steps.body: State 07. The group assembly lens for multi-entity reporting, eliminations, and consolidation adjustments.

pillar_sections.title: Operational consequences of the distinction
paragraphs: ERP consequence: consolidation systems may reshape, eliminate, or reclassify submitted reporting packages.
Close consequence: entity close and group close require separate evidence and timing controls.
Reconciliation consequence: consolidation bridges must tie entity reporting submissions to group adjustments.
Reporting consequence: group FX and elimination narratives weaken when entity reporting currency is treated as the final consolidated lens.

pillar_sections.title: Where confusion creates risk
paragraphs: Confusion creates risk when entity reporting packs are summed as if they were already consolidated truth.
The failure pattern is a group statement that cannot explain eliminations, ownership effects, or consolidation adjustments by source entity.

pillar_sections.title: Questions each concept must answer
paragraphs: These questions are reference prompts for evidence discipline. They are not audit, accounting, tax, legal, treasury, or ERP implementation advice.
questions: Reporting currency custody question: which entity package proves how measured results were presented?
Consolidation currency custody question: which group bridge proves eliminations, ownership effects, and consolidation adjustments?
Bridge question: can each consolidated line be traced back to entity packages and approved consolidation journals?

pillar_sections.title: Glossary, state, and methodology links
paragraphs: The related references keep the comparison inside AccountCcy.com terminology and make the trust boundary explicit. The methodology link explains claim limits and source discipline.

pillar_sections.title: Practical comparison questions
questions: Is consolidation currency just group reporting currency? It is the group assembly lens, which includes elimination and ownership mechanics beyond entity presentation.
Can reporting currency and consolidation currency be the same code? Yes, but the governance layer is different.
Where does risk appear? In eliminations, intercompany balances, ownership changes, and group-level FX explanations.

graph_title: Keep comparison intent inside the custody chain
graph_intro: The purpose of this page is to strengthen professional search intent while preserving AccountCcy.com claim discipline and state architecture.

cards.label: State position
cards.title: Each term belongs to a different control layer.
cards.body: The distinction is strongest when mapped to the CCY State Chain rather than reduced to screen labels.

cards.label: Evidence
cards.title: Different evidence proves different currency identities.
cards.body: Configuration, payment, ledger, translation, and consolidation artifacts should not be substituted for each other.

cards.label: Trust boundary
cards.title: The page is a reference framework, not advice.
cards.body: The methodology page defines claim limits, source boundaries, and publication discipline.

control_title: Index entity packages to consolidation journals
control_body: Maintain bridges from entity reporting submissions to consolidation adjustments, eliminations, ownership entries, and group reporting lines.
risk_title: Entity presentation mistaken for consolidated truth
risk_body: The risk is a consolidated narrative that cannot be reconstructed from entity submissions and group adjustment evidence.
